meaning: n.饶舌,多嘴,爱说话vi.空谈,唠叨,瞎扯
examples: I have the gift of gab, so I'd make a good salesperson.
Fat people will gab, giggle, guffaw, gallumph, gyrate and gossip.
"Nibs, would—Stow this gab," roared Hook, and the spokesmen were dragged back.
Tremor looks for kids with a wide social circle and a gift of gab.
This can mean more hours studying in the library and fewer gab fests at the student union.
